name: Spotify Web API
what_it_does: Provides access to Spotify's music catalog, user library, playlists, playback controls, and audio analysis features for building music-aware applications and automation.
use_cases: Programmatically create and populate playlists based on mood or activity context detected by an agent
Fetch audio features (tempo, energy, valence, danceability) for tracks to build a music recommendation engine
Control active Spotify playback — skip tracks, adjust volume, or queue songs — as part of a smart environment agent
Monitor a user's recently played tracks and top artists to generate personalized weekly music digest reports
Search the catalog and retrieve track metadata to enrich a music knowledge base or trivia bot
not_for: Downloading or redistributing audio content — the API provides streaming control and metadata, not audio file downloads
Playback control on free-tier accounts — several player endpoints require an active Spotify Premium subscription
Building competing music streaming services — Spotify's ToS explicitly prohibits using the API to replicate core streaming functionality
best_when: Building music-aware agents, playlist automation tools, or ambient computing integrations for users with active Spotify accounts, especially Premium subscribers for full playback control.
avoid_when: Your users are on Spotify free tier and you need player control, or you need to stream or download audio files rather than just control playback.

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 with 13+ scopes covering playlist management, user library, listening history, playback control, user profile, and follow actions. Client Credentials flow available for catalog searches that don't require user context. Authorization Code with PKCE recommended for user-facing apps. Tokens expire in 1 hour and must be refreshed.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Developer access is free with up to 25 users in Development mode. Extended quota requires submitting for quota extension review.
pricing.paid_tiers: No direct API payment tiers; API access is free but usage is governed by quota extension approval for apps with many users
pricing.notes: Apps in Development mode are limited to 25 users. To serve more users, you must apply for a quota extension, which requires Spotify review and approval. Spotify has tightened API access in recent years, restricting some endpoints (e.g., audio features) for new apps.

performance.rate_limits: Approximately 30 requests per second per app. Rate limit headers (Retry-After) are returned on 429 responses. Web Playback SDK has separate limits.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Excellent documentation with interactive reference, code samples, and explicit error code tables. 401/403/429 errors include actionable reason strings. Premium-required errors are clearly indicated per endpoint.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: GET endpoints are fully idempotent. PUT/POST playlist and library mutations are generally safe to retry but lack idempotency keys. Adding the same track to a playlist twice creates a duplicate.
known_agent_gotchas: Player endpoints (pause, skip, queue) require an active Spotify Premium subscription AND an active device — calls against free accounts or with no active device return 403/404 with misleading error messages
Audio features endpoint has been restricted for new apps as of 2024 — agents should not assume access to acoustic analysis data without verifying app permissions
OAuth tokens expire after 1 hour; agents running long background workflows must proactively refresh tokens or face mid-run 401 failures
Adding duplicate tracks to a playlist is allowed and not idempotent — agents performing playlist sync must deduplicate before writing
Rate limiting is per-app globally, not per-endpoint — a burst on search can exhaust the budget for concurrent playback control calls in the same agent session

security_score_components.security_notes: Very granular OAuth scope system allows least-privilege access. PKCE support for public clients eliminates client secret exposure risk. Client Credentials flow for non-user data avoids unnecessary user token usage. Well-maintained official SDKs reduce implementation error risk.
